papers.title: WorldDrive: Unified World Model for End-to-End Autonomous Driving with Diffusion Policy
papers.summary: This paper introduces WorldDrive, a novel framework that combines world models with diffusion policies for end-to-end autonomous driving. The approach learns a latent world model to predict future states and uses a diffusion policy to generate safe and smooth trajectories. Experiments on CARLA and nuScenes show state-of-the-art performance in both open-loop and closed-loop evaluations.

papers.title: VLA-Transformer: Vision-Language-Action Transformer for Multi-Modal Driving
papers.summary: We present VLA-Transformer, a unified transformer architecture for vision-language-action tasks in autonomous driving. The model processes camera images, LiDAR point clouds, and language instructions simultaneously, generating control commands through a shared latent space. The approach achieves strong performance on Waymo Open Dataset and shows excellent zero-shot generalization.

papers.title: BEVFormer++: Enhanced Bird's Eye View Perception with Temporal Fusion
papers.summary: BEVFormer++ extends the BEVFormer architecture with improved temporal fusion mechanisms for better perception in autonomous driving. The model incorporates multi-scale feature aggregation and adaptive attention to handle dynamic scenes more effectively. Results show significant improvements on nuScenes benchmark.

papers.title: Diffusion Policy for Robust Autonomous Driving in Adverse Conditions
papers.summary: This paper explores diffusion policies for autonomous driving under adverse weather conditions. The proposed method learns to generate diverse and safe trajectories by modeling the planning process as a denoising diffusion process. Experiments in CARLA with rain and fog show improved robustness compared to traditional methods.

papers.title: Imitation Learning with Safety Constraints for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: We propose a constrained imitation learning framework that incorporates safety constraints directly into the learning process. The method uses Lagrangian multipliers to balance imitation accuracy and safety violations, resulting in policies that are both performant and safe. Evaluation on urban driving scenarios demonstrates effectiveness.

papers.title: Learning Vision-Language-Action World Models for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have recently achieved notable progress in end-to-end autonomous driving by integrating perception, reasoning, and control within a unified multimodal framework. However, they often lack explicit modeling of temporal dynamics and global world consistency, which limits their foresight and safety. In contrast, world models can simulate plausible future scenes but generally struggle to reason about or evaluate the imagined future they generate. In this work, we p...

papers.title: Scaling-Aware Data Selection for End-to-End Autonomous Driving Systems
papers.summary: Large-scale deep learning models for physical AI applications depend on diverse training data collection efforts. These models and correspondingly, the training data, must address different evaluation criteria necessary for the models to be deployable in real-world environments. Data selection policies can guide the development of the training set, but current frameworks do not account for the ambiguity in how data points affect different metrics. In this work, we propose Mixture Optimization vi...
